mission.title: alertmanager: Allow negative (re) matching in silences
mission.description: Allow negative (re) matching in silences. Requested by 19+ users.

steps.title: Check current alertmanager deployment
steps.description:
Verify your alertmanager version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n alertmanager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=alertmanager
helm list -n alertmanager 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working alertmanager installation.

steps.title: Review alertmanager configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant alertmanager configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n alertmanager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=alertmanager
kubectl get configmap -n alertmanager -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=alertmanager
```
Similar to https://github.com/prometheus/alertmanager/issues/1023, but for silences.

Currently you can only have `=` and `=~` matchers in silences.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Allow negative (re) matching in silences
steps.description:
ref #1575
This PR adds support for assuming the first label is `alertname` in silence add and query. It was already existing on silence query, did some cleanup.

Will create another PR for #1682
```yaml
$ amtool silence add job !~".+exporter" instance="someinstance"
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n alertmanager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=alertmanager
kubectl get events -n alertmanager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Allow negative (re) matching in silences" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
ref #1575
This PR adds support for assuming the first label is `alertname` in silence add and query. It was already existing on silence query, did some cleanup.

Will create another PR for #1682
codeSnippets:
$ ./amtool silence query foo
is same as
$ ./amtool silence query alertname=foo

and

$ ./amtool silence query ~foo
is same as
$ ./amtool silence query alertname=~foo

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with alertmanager installed or the issue environment reproducible.
